rationale: The citing paper does more than list this source as background: it repeatedly uses Thomas, Blattberg, and Fox (2004) as a key prior study on customer reacquisition ("recapturing lost customers"). It is cited to justify a modeling assumption that new and recaptured customers are not separated in diffusion influence parameters, and later as a rare empirical exception in a literature with limited reacquisition evidence. Although the paper does not directly reuse the cited paper's method, the citation meaningfully informs its conceptual setup around attrition and return behavior.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited work as prior empirical grounding for the reacquisition component of its attrition framework, especially when motivating assumptions about recaptured versus first-time adopters. It positions Thomas et al. (2004) as an important reference point in a sparse reacquisition literature.
